name: Coedit-XL High-Scoring NAEP Math Response Augmentation
population_or_context: Ten NAEP 2023 math scoring challenge constructed-response items from grade 4 and grade 8 students.
sample_size: Training dataset of 251,370 responses across ten items; held-out test dataset of 27,930 responses.
methods: The team filtered overrepresented low-scoring responses, generated paraphrases of high-scoring responses with Coedit-XL, modified multi-step inputs, and fine-tuned item-specific transformer scorers.
education_context: Mathematics constructed-response assessment, including short constructed responses and extended constructed responses in multi-step NAEP items.
response_origin: Hybrid: authentic NAEP responses plus Coedit-XL paraphrases of authentic higher-scoring target responses.
rubric_label_space: Item-specific score scales with target response scores ranging from 1 to item maxima between 2 and 5.
label_source: NAEP human-scored training data; augmented paraphrases were generated from authentic responses with scores 2 or 3 for underrepresented higher-score classes.
generation_method: Large-language-model paraphrase augmentation of existing higher-scoring student responses, with added spelling/lowercase perturbations to resemble student submissions.
prompt_strategy: Coedit-XL paraphrasing with beam search and diversity penalty; up to eight augmented responses per authentic response.
evaluation_method: Submitted and internally evaluated fine-tuned models against human-scored held-out responses using quadratic weighted kappa and demographic bias analyses.
scorer_use: Training augmentation, label balancing, scorer benchmarking, and bias screening in a scoring challenge.
real_response_comparator: Yes. Final predictions were evaluated against withheld NAEP human scores and compared with human-human agreement thresholds.
sparsity_or_imbalance_motivation: Several target items had excess low-scoring responses and too few higher-scoring responses, especially scores 2 and 3.
synthetic_data_research_role: Generated additional high-scoring constructed responses as training data to balance labels before scorer fine-tuning.
reported_metrics: Human-like agreement within QWK 0.05 of human-human agreement on nine of ten held-out items; standardized mean difference checks for bias.
limitations: The pipeline was item-specific, competition-oriented, and still had weaker performance on at least one item where imbalance and missing contextual information were problematic.
